bids.reasoning: I am targeting a maximum-utility opening bid since time is at the very start and there is no need to concede yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall despite their “balanced” message, though it does reveal compatibility on issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, so I keep those where they also work well for me.

I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid (1.00) because it is still very early at t=0.02 and there is no need to concede yet. The opponent has repeated the same valid but low-utility offer for me, and their “balanced” language is not supported by the numbers, so I am holding firm while varying my previous bid slightly on issueD, where valueA and valueB are equally good for me.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than conceding on high-weight issues. The opponent’s offers give me low utility and have not improved; their messages claim fairness, but the numbers show they are holding firm or even worsening. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by switching issueD to valueB, which is equally valuable to me.

I am targeting near-maximum utility because it is still very early (t=0.06) and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; in fact their latest bid is worse because issueD moved to valueC. Their messages claim balance and fairness, but the actual offers heavily concede only issueE while asking me to give up on several high-weight issues, so I will hold firm on my key preferences while avoiding an exact repeat of my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than making costly concessions. The opponent’s offers remain weak for me on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, with only movement on issueD, which is not costly for me because valueA and valueB are equally best there. Their “win-win” messages are cheap talk, so I am relying on the actual bids and avoiding a repeat of my immediately previous offer by using issueD: valueB.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.94 because it is still early (t=0.10) and the opponent has mostly held firm on low-value-to-me positions, with only movement on issueD. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk; the actual offers show they strongly prefer valueC on issueA/B/C/F, so I am making small concessions on issueA and issueF while holding my most important/high-value positions.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF, while moving on issueA and varying issueD; I am accepting their workable issueD=valueB and giving a small concession on low-weight issueF, but holding my high-value positions on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need to concede on my high-weight issues B and C yet. The opponent’s actual offers show movement on issueA and issueD but continued firmness on issueB, issueC, and issueF; I can concede issueF first because it is lower weight for me, while matching their latest acceptable-looking positions on issueA, issueD, and issueE.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while making a limited concession on low-weight issueF. The opponent’s offers remain poor for me overall, though they have signaled willingness on issueA and sometimes issueD, so I keep my high-weight priorities on issueB and issueC while using issueD=valueA to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me on issueA and issueC but they continue to hold firm on issueB and issueF, so I concede on issueF and use issueD=valueB, which is costless to me and often appears in their bids, while holding my high-weight priorities on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.20, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s actual offers, despite “win-win” language, still hold firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF, but they have shown willingness to give issueA and issueD; I will match them on issueA/issueD while keeping my high-weight priorities on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a strong but slightly more accommodating bid around 0.844 utility by conceding on issueC to valueB and keeping the already-conceded issueF at valueC. Time is still early-mid, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility packages yet; their offers show some movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD, but they continue to demand poor values on issueB and issueF, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84 because we are only at t=0.24, so there is no need to drop near the opponent’s current offers yet. The opponent’s bids show some movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD, but they remain firm on issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC; I can concede issueF and partially concede issueC while holding my higher-value positions on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.844: conceding on issueF and partially on issueC while holding issueB, which is still high-value for me. I am targeting a utility around 0.82, which is still strong for me while making mid-game concessions on issueA, issueC, and issueF. At t=0.44 there is some time pressure to move beyond my maximal bids, and the opponent’s actual offers show persistent demand for issueF: valueC and issueE: valueA, with some movement toward me on issueB/issueC/issueD despite their repetitive “win-win” messaging.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier top bids but still preserves value on the higher-weight issues. At t=0.50 there is no need to drop to the opponent’s latest low-utility package yet, but their recent offer shows movement toward issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueC, so I am only pushing back on issueD with valueB, which they have offered several times before.
